findings.evidence: Grep: `Acceptance.lean:37 rw [normalizedChebyshevT_def, chebyshevTNormSq_of_ne_zero (by norm_num)]`; existing general input is `Measure.lean:71 lemma chebyshevTNormSq_of_ne_zero {n : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0)` and `Measure.lean:222 lemma coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp ... (n : ℕ)`.
findings.fix: State the general positive-degree lemmas first, e.g. `normalizedChebyshevT_of_ne_zero_apply {n} (hn : n ≠ 0) (x) : normalizedChebyshevT n x = (T ℝ n).eval x / Real.sqrt (Real.pi / 2)` and the matching `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_of_ne_zero`; derive the `one` lemmas as special cases if named acceptance aliases are still wanted.
findings.issue: `normalizedChebyshevT_one_apply` and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_one` specialize the positive-degree normalization to `1`, even though the proof only uses `chebyshevTNormSq_of_ne_zero`.

summary: The degree-one normalized-mode API is under-generalized: the same denominator simplification holds for every nonzero Chebyshev degree, and the `L²` representative lemma repeats that special case.
